what: what are data types in javascript
how many data types does javascript have
why: why array is object in js
compare: is null a number or object

1_beginner_explanation.summary: JavaScript has 8 built-in data types grouped into two categories: Primitives (immutable, stored directly by value: string, number, bigint, boolean, undefined, null, symbol) and Reference types (mutable, stored by reference address: objects, arrays, functions). Check a variable's type at runtime using the 'typeof' operator.
1_beginner_explanation.analogy: Primitive data types are like coins — if you want to give one to someone, you copy/hand over the coin itself. If you scratch a copy of a coin, the original remains untouched. Reference types are like Google Docs links — if you share a link with a friend, both of you can modify the same single document in the cloud.

2_internal_mechanism.execution_flow: JavaScript is dynamically typed, meaning variable bindings are not bound to a specific type, but values are. Type checking occurs at runtime rather than compile-time.
2_internal_mechanism.memory: Primitive values reside on the Stack (fixed size, fast access). Reference objects reside on the Heap (dynamic size), and their memory address pointers are saved on the Stack.

3_real_world_example.use_case: Using 'null' to intentionally denote the absence of a value (such as clearing user search results), and checking variable types dynamically.
3_real_world_example.code:
let activeUser = null; // Intentional empty state
activeUser = { name: 'Ali', role: 'admin' }; // Replaced with object reference
console.log(typeof activeUser); // 'object'

4_common_confusion.trap: Confusing 'undefined' (variable is declared but hasn't been given a value yet) with 'null' (value is explicitly set to signify nothingness).
4_common_confusion.fix: Use null when you want to clear/reset a variable value explicitly. Let JS handle undefined as the implicit default state.

6_edge_case.quirk: 'typeof null' returns 'object'. This is a historical bug in the first version of JavaScript, where values were represented by a type tag and value in binary. The object type tag was 000, and since null was represented by the NULL pointer (all zeroes), it was misidentified as an object.
6_edge_case.code:
console.log(typeof null); // 'object' ️ (legacy JS bug)
console.log(typeof []);   // 'object' ️ (arrays are sub-types of object)
console.log(Array.isArray([])); // true  (use this to check arrays correctly)
